@graph.name: Land Rover models fitted with the Volvo SI6 (B6324S)

mainEntity.name: How much does an SI6 (B6324S) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned SI6 3.2 typically costs £2,200–£3,500 supply only, with used units from £800–£1,600. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

mainEntity.name: Is the Volvo SI6 (B6324S) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the SI6 is considered a reliable and refined petrol engine (4.0★). Its smooth inline-six design offers excellent power delivery and durability. However, early examples (2006–2009) have reported timing chain tensioner issues, so service history is critical.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common SI6 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure is the most critical — particularly on early engines (2006–2009) where the tensioner design was prone to failure, causing chain stretch and catastrophic valve/piston interference. Oil consumption and leaks from the timing cover are also reported.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the SI6?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Land Rover application, the SI6 (B6324S) powered the Freelander 2 (L359) from 2006 to 2014. The engine was also used across Volvo's XC60, XC90, S80, and V70 models.

mainEntity.name: How long does the SI6 (B6324S) last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance and early timing chain tensioner replacement where needed, SI6 engines routinely exceed 150,000–200,000 miles. Regular oil changes and chain inspections are key to achieving this lifespan, particularly on pre-2010 examples.
